reasoning_chain_text: 1. For school years 2015-2016, 2016-2017, 2017-2018, and 2018-2019, identify NYC middle schools with at least 50 specialized high school offers and keep schools that meet the threshold every year.
2. From the remaining schools, keep those located in Queens District 26.
3. For the candidate school namesakes, determine which are scientists and compare the scientists by birth date to identify the older one.
4. Determine the founding year of the research institute founded and first directed by the older scientist.
original_final_question: Which New York City middle schools located in Queens District 26 had (1) at least 50 offers to specialized high schools on specialized high school admissions test in every school year from 2015-19, and (2) are named after scientists. In what year was the research institute founded and first directed by the older of these two scientists? Please return the year only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: Node 1: SHSAT offers SY2015-16 >= 50 -> 27 schools (including 4 District 26 schools)
Node 2: SHSAT offers SY2016-17 >= 50 -> 27 schools (including 4 District 26 schools)
Node 3: SHSAT offers SY2017-18 >= 50 -> 28 schools (including 4 District 26 schools)
Node 4: SHSAT offers SY2018-19 >= 50 -> 23 schools (including 4 District 26 schools)
Intersection of nodes 1-4: Schools meeting criterion all 4 years -> 21 schools
Node 5 (School Locations): Filter to Queens District 26 -> 4 schools (Pasteur, Hawthorne, Curie, Ryan)
Node 6 (Wikipedia Louis_Pasteur): Pasteur was a scientist, born December 27, 1822
Node 7 (Wikipedia Marie_Curie): Curie was a scientist, born November 7, 1867
Node 8 (Wikipedia Nathaniel_Hawthorne): Hawthorne was NOT a scientist (novelist)
Note: George J. Ryan has no Wikipedia article and cannot be verified as a scientist
Filter: Scientists only -> Pasteur (born 1822), Curie (born 1867) = 2 scientists
Superlative: Older (earlier birth year) -> Pasteur (1822) vs Curie (1867) -> Louis Pasteur is older
Node 9: Louis Pasteur founded the Pasteur Institute in 1887 and was its first director until his death

ideal_code.intent: Which NYC middle schools had at least 50 SHSAT offers to specialized high schools in school year 2015-16?
ideal_code.code:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = 'datagov/2015-2016-shsat-admissions-test-offers-by-sending-school/files/rows.txt'
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)
offers = pd.to_numeric(df['Count of Offers'], errors="coerce")
filtered = df[offers >= 50].copy()
lookup = {row["Feeder School Name"]: (row["Feeder School DBN"], int(pd.to_numeric(row['Count of Offers'], errors="coerce"))) for _, row in filtered.iterrows()}
answer = {}
for school in ["J.H.S. 067 Louis Pasteur", "M.S. 158 Marie Curie", "J.H.S. 074 Nathaniel Hawthorne", "J.H.S. 216 George J. Ryan"]:
    dbn, count = lookup[school]
    answer[school] = f"{dbn}, {count} offers" if True else dbn
answer['and 23 others'] = "..."

ideal_code.intent: Which of the 21 schools with >= 50 SHSAT offers in all 4 years are located in Queens District 26?
ideal_code.code:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/2016-2017-school-locations/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)
schools = ["J.H.S. 067 Louis Pasteur", "J.H.S. 074 Nathaniel Hawthorne", "M.S. 158 Marie Curie", "J.H.S. 216 George J. Ryan"]
filtered = df[df["LOCATION_NAME"].isin(schools)].copy()
filtered["GEOGRAPHICAL_DISTRICT_CODE"] = pd.to_numeric(filtered["GEOGRAPHICAL_DISTRICT_CODE"], errors="coerce")
assert set(filtered["GEOGRAPHICAL_DISTRICT_CODE"].dropna().astype(int)) == {26}
lookup = dict(zip(filtered["LOCATION_NAME"], filtered["ATS_SYSTEM_CODE"]))
answer = {school: f"{lookup[school]}, District 26" for school in schools}

ideal_query.intent: Which NYC middle schools had at least 50 SHSAT offers to specialized high schools in school year 2015-16?
ideal_query.sql:
WITH filtered AS (
  SELECT upper(trim("Feeder School Name")) AS school_key, "Feeder School DBN" || ', ' || CAST(TRY_CAST("Count of Offers" AS BIGINT) AS VARCHAR) || ' offers' AS val
  FROM t
  WHERE TRY_CAST("Count of Offers" AS BIGINT) >= 50
), vals AS (
  SELECT 'J.H.S. 067 Louis Pasteur' AS key, max(CASE WHEN school_key = 'J.H.S. 067 LOUIS PASTEUR' THEN val END) AS value FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'M.S. 158 Marie Curie' AS key, max(CASE WHEN school_key = 'M.S. 158 MARIE CURIE' THEN val END) AS value FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'J.H.S. 074 Nathaniel Hawthorne' AS key, max(CASE WHEN school_key = 'J.H.S. 074 NATHANIEL HAWTHORNE' THEN val END) AS value FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'J.H.S. 216 George J. Ryan' AS key, max(CASE WHEN school_key = 'J.H.S. 216 GEORGE J. RYAN' THEN val END) AS value FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'and 23 others', '...'
)
SELECT to_json(map(list(key), list(value))) AS answer FROM vals

ideal_query.intent: Which of the 21 schools with >= 50 SHSAT offers in all 4 years are located in Queens District 26?
ideal_query.sql:
WITH vals AS (
  SELECT LOCATION_NAME AS key, trim("ATS SYSTEM CODE") || ', District ' || CAST(TRY_CAST(GEOGRAPHICAL_DISTRICT_CODE AS BIGINT) AS VARCHAR) AS value
  FROM t
  WHERE LOCATION_NAME IN ('J.H.S. 067 Louis Pasteur', 'J.H.S. 074 Nathaniel Hawthorne', 'M.S. 158 Marie Curie', 'J.H.S. 216 George J. Ryan')
    AND TRY_CAST(GEOGRAPHICAL_DISTRICT_CODE AS BIGINT) = 26
)
SELECT to_json(map(list(key), list(value))) AS answer FROM vals
